'''National Food Chain Safety Office''' (Nébih/NFCSO) is the integrated food safety authority of [[Hungary]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ecolex.org/details/legislation/act-no-xlvi-of-2008-on-food-chain-and-its-control-lex-faoc098048/|title=Act No. XLVI of 2008 on food chain and its control.|website=www.ecolex.org|language=en|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> established on 15 March 2012.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://portal.nebih.gov.hu/hivatalunk/bemutatkozas/ismerje-meg-hivatalunkat|title=Ismerje meg hivatalunkat - Nébih|website=portal.nebih.gov.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://net.jogtar.hu/jr/gen/getdoc2.cgi?dbnum=1&docid=A1200022.KOR|title=22/2012. (II. 29.) Korm. rendelet - a Nemzeti Élelmiszerlánc-biztonsági Hivatalról|last=Kft.|first=Wolters Kluwer|access-date=2017-05-08|language=en}}</ref>

== History ==
The roots of NFCSO are dated back to the first officially organized animal health service<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://agraragazat.hu/hir/vm-125-eves-allategeszsegugyi-szolgalat|title=VM: 125 éves az állategészségügyi szolgálat|website=agraragazat.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> founded by the Animal Health Act VII/1888.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://1000ev.hu/index.php?a=3&param=6285|title=1888. évi VII. törvénycikk {{!}} 1000 év törvényei|website=1000ev.hu|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> The focus of the service has shifted from animal health to [[food security]], then [[food safety]] according to the historical needs of Hungary. Sustainability issues and investigation of cases related to economic frauds<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://net.jogtar.hu/jr/gen/hjegy_doc.cgi?docid=a0900095.tv|title=2009. évi XCV. törvény - a mezőgazdasági és élelmiszeripari termékek vonatkozásában a beszállítókkal szemben alkalmazott tisztességtelen forgalmazói magatartás tilalmáról|last=Kft.|first=Wolters Kluwer|access-date=2017-05-10|language=en}}</ref> in the food chain has appeared in the 21st century.

NFCSO is the direct legal successor of the Central Agricultural Office (CAO) and the [[Hungarian Food Safety Office]]. The CAO was the first food chain control authority in Hungary as the general legal successor of the [[Plant and Soil]] Protection Services, the Central Service of Plant and Soil Protection, the Animal Health and Food Control Stations, the National Institute for Agricultural Quality Control, the National Forestry Service, the Agricultural Budget Office, the National Wine Qualification Institute, the Institute of Veterinary Vaccine-, Medicine- and Feed Control, the National Animal Health Institute and the National Food Control Institute, which institutions had worked independently before 1 January 2007.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nebih.gov.hu/en/|title=Introduction of the National Food Chain Safety Office (NFCSO)|date=1970-01-01|website=www.nebih.gov.hu|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref>

== Responsibilities ==
From 2012 NFCSO is responsible for the control of the [[whole food]] chain from farm to fork, including soil protection, agricultural production, forestry, [[food processing]], retail and catering. Besides this, the most significant suppliers of the food chain are also registered by NFCSO (for instance private laboratories, input material suppliers).<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://net.jogtar.hu/jr/gen/hjegy_doc.cgi?docid=a0800046.tv|title=2008. évi XLVI. törvény - az élelmiszerláncról és hatósági felügyeletéről|last=Kft.|first=Wolters Kluwer|access-date=2017-05-08|language=en|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170329033355/https://net.jogtar.hu/jr/gen/hjegy_doc.cgi?docid=a0800046.tv|archive-date=2017-03-29}}</ref> The National Food Chain Safety Office operates the food chain laboratory system and the national reference laboratory network.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://portal.nebih.gov.hu/kapcsolat/laboratoriumok/nebih-laboratoriumok|title=NÉBIH laboratóriumok - Nébih|website=portal.nebih.gov.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref>

Most of NFCSO's directorates pursue academic activities ranging from life sciences to social sciences. The institution operates two external university departments together with the University of Veterinary Medicine, Budapest and [[Hungarian University of Agriculture and Life Sciences]], Institute of Food Science and Technology. Typical research areas include methodological development of laboratory investigations, risk assessment, food chain network analysis, food consumption patterns and risk perception of consumers.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Süth |first1=M. |last2=Mikulka |first2=P. |last3=Izsó |first3=T. |last4=Kasza |first4=Gy |date=2018-09-01 |title=Possibilities of targeting in food chain safety risk communication |url=https://akjournals.com/view/journals/066/47/3/article-p307.xml |journal=Acta Alimentaria |language=en |volume=47 |issue=3 |pages=307–314 |doi=10.1556/066.2018.47.3.6 |s2cid=105287803 |issn=1588-2535}}</ref> A significant share of the research projects are conducted in cooperation with international and national research institutions and universities.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.univet.hu/hu/egyetem/esemenyek/5|title=Állatorvostudományi Egyetem, Budapest - Egyetemi és hatósági összefogás a hatékony utánpótlás nevelésért|website=www.univet.hu|language=hu|access-date=2017-08-22}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://szie.hu/kihelyezett-tanszeket-alapitott-az-egyetem-es-nebih|title=Kihelyezett tanszéket alapított az egyetem és a NÉBIH {{!}} SZIE|website=szie.hu|access-date=2017-08-22}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://cordis.europa.eu/project/rcn/210495_en.html|title=European Commission : CORDIS : Projects and Results: SafeConsumE: Safer food through changed consumer behavior: Effective tools and products, communication strategies, education and a food safety policy reducing health burden from foodborne illnesses|website=cordis.europa.eu|access-date=2017-08-22}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://safeconsume.eu/|title=SafeConsumE project official webpage}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/data/food-consumption-data|title=Food consumption data {{!}} European Food Safety Authority|website=www.efsa.europa.eu|language=en|access-date=2017-08-22}}</ref>

The work of the directorates is coordinated by the president of NFCSO, also acting as deputy chief veterinary officer of Hungary. The activities of NFCSO are supervised by the Ministry of Agriculture and personally the [[Chief veterinary officer (Hungary)|chief veterinary officer of Hungary]].

The basic principle of organizing the activities of NFCSO is [[food safety risk analysis]] as defined by the [[Codex Alimentarius Commission]]. Accordingly, besides food risk assessment and risk management, the Office also deals with food safety [[risk communication]] (both preventive and crisis communication)<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Kasza |first1=Gyula |last2=Csenki |first2=Eszter |last3=Szakos |first3=Dávid |last4=Izsó |first4=Tekla |date=2022-08-01 |title=The evolution of food safety risk communication: Models and trends in the past and the future |journal=Food Control |language=en |volume=138 |article-number=109025 |doi=10.1016/j.foodcont.2022.109025 |s2cid=248223805 |issn=0956-7135|doi-access=free }}</ref> and also consumer related programmes, such as campaigns for conscious shopping,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eteltcsakokosan.hu/|title=Ételt csak OKOSAN – Élelmiszerbiztonságról mindenkinek|website=eteltcsakokosan.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> education programmes,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kormany.hu/en/ministry-of-agriculture/news/nfcso-complex-education-program-a-success|title=NFCSO complex education program a success|website=Government|access-date=2019-06-18}}</ref> responsible animal keeping,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://szabadagazdi.hu/|title=Szabad a gazdi - Program a felelős állattartásért|website=Szabad a gazdi|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> reducing [[antimicrobial resistance]],<ref>{{Cite web|title=Meddighat – Mi az antibiotikum-rezisztencia?|url=https://meddighat.hu/|access-date=2021-07-09|language=hu}}</ref> prevention of [[food waste]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://maradeknelkul.hu/|title=maradeknelkul.hu – … maradék nélkül …|website=maradeknelkul.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> prevention of [[Wildfire|forest fires]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://erdotuz.hu/life/|title=FIRELIFE|website=Erdotuz.hu|language=hu-HU|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref>

Since 2016, NFCSO has been the organiser of the Hungarian national level food waste prevention programme, called [http://maradeknelkul.hu Wasteless]. The coordinator of the programme is the official representative of Hungary in the [https://ec.europa.eu/food/food/food-waste/eu-actions-against-food-waste/eu-platform-food-losses-and-food-waste_en EU Platform on Food Losses and Food Waste], hosted by the [[Directorate-General for Health and Food Safety|EU Commission's Directorate-General for Health and Food Safety]]. Besides awareness raising and education,<ref>{{Cite book|title=Achievements and Statistics of Project Wasteless|publisher=NFCSO|year=2021|location=Budapest}}</ref> NFCSO also conducts research activities in this field.<ref>{{Cite web|title=PUBLICATIONS & MEDIA – Maradéknélkül.hu|url=http://maradeknelkul.hu/en/publications-and-media/|access-date=2021-07-08|language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Szabó-Bódi |first1=Barbara |last2=Kasza |first2=Gyula |last3=Szakos |first3=Dávid |date=2018-01-01 |title=Assessment of household food waste in Hungary |journal=British Food Journal |volume=120 |issue=3 |pages=625–638 |doi=10.1108/BFJ-04-2017-0255 |issn=0007-070X}}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Kasza |first1=Gyula |last2=Dorkó |first2=Annamária |last3=Kunszabó |first3=Atilla |last4=Szakos |first4=Dávid |date=January 2020 |title=Quantification of Household Food Waste in Hungary: A Replication Study Using the FUSIONS Methodology |journal=Sustainability |language=en |volume=12 |issue=8 |page=3069 |doi=10.3390/su12083069 |issn=2071-1050|doi-access=free }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Kasza |first1=Gyula |last2=Szabó-Bódi |first2=Barbara |last3=Lakner |first3=Zoltán |last4=Izsó |first4=Tekla |date=2019-02-01 |title=Balancing the desire to decrease food waste with requirements of food safety |url=https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0924224417303709 |journal=Trends in Food Science & Technology |series=Keeping Food Safety on the Agenda for 15 years – The SAFE consortium |language=en |volume=84 |pages=74–76 |doi=10.1016/j.tifs.2018.07.019 |s2cid=91373380 |issn=0924-2244|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== National and international relations ==
NFCSO is the official contact point to the [[European Food Safety Authority]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.efsa.europa.eu/en/people/fpmembers|title=Focal Point members and observers {{!}} European Food Safety Authority|website=www.efsa.europa.eu|language=en|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> and the [[Rapid Alert System for Food and Feed]],<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://ec.europa.eu/food/safety/rasff/members_en#15|title=Members of RASFF network - Food Safety - European Commission|website=Food Safety|language=en|access-date=2017-05-08}}</ref> and the [[Foodborne illness#International Food Safety Authorities Network .28INFOSAN.29|International Food Safety Authorities Network]]. Officially linked to the [[Minister of Agriculture (Hungary)|Ministry of Agriculture of Hungary]] and the [[European Commission]]'s [[Directorate-General for Health and Food Safety]] (DG SANTE),  the [[World Health Organization]] (WHO)  and [[Food and Agriculture Organization|FAO]]/WHO [[Codex Alimentarius]].
